description: the birds were medium sized finches - notcieably larger than nearby Pine Siskins, but much smaller than a Pine Grosbeak. I only looked at the birds long enough to be sure they were Red Crossbills, but I saw the crossed bill, the brick red coloration of the one adult male I could see, and black wings with no white wingbars.  See below for description of the call.Behaviors: the birds were first seen flying into the top of an engalmann spruce. They fed upon cones there for a couple of minutes and then flew out, at which point I obtained the recordings of the call.Call: see belowPlumage: alternate
photographer: Photographer: Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer
